candidates.why: Represents the main operating top-line; matches Total Revenue numerically and is explicitly labeled as revenue.
Explicit total revenue line with same figures as Operating Revenue; best single top-line label.
Finance income, not operating revenue; should not be used for REVT.
Represents net non-operating result, not revenue.
Bottom-line comprehensive income, not revenue.
Bottom-line comprehensive income, not revenue.

candidates.row_label: Contract Assets - Short-Term - Unbilled Revenue in Excess of Advance Consideration & Progress Billings
candidates.why: Balance sheet contract asset; not the income statement revenue line.

candidates.row_label: Contract Liabilities - Short-Term - Advance Consideration & Progress Billings in Excess of Unbilled Revenue
candidates.why: Balance sheet contract liability; not revenue.

variables.notes: Using Total Revenue as primary top-line, with Operating Revenue as fallback since they are equivalent in this preview.

candidates.why: Major operating cost likely including direct labor; best available proxy for COGS in a services-oriented company with no explicit cost of sales line.

candidates.row_label: Stock-Based Compensation in Selling, General & Administrative Expenses
candidates.why: SG&A stock-based comp; overhead not specifically COGS, better suited for XSGA than COGS.
Broad operating cost bucket likely including direct service costs and overhead; reasonable part of COGS proxy when no explicit cost of sales exists.
Represents net operating expenses, not a pure cost of sales component, and mixes multiple items.
Financing cost, not operating cost of sales.
Balance sheet liability, not a P&L cost line.

variables.notes: No explicit cost of sales line; using main operating cost components (personnel and other operating expenses) as the best available proxy for COGS.

candidates.why: Core operating personnel costs; typical SG&A component in absence of a distinct SG&A line.

candidates.row_label: Stock-Based Compensation in Selling, General & Administrative Expenses
candidates.why: Explicitly labeled SG&A stock-based compensation; should be included as an SG&A component.
Bucket of operating overhead costs; appropriate SG&A component when no single SG&A line is given.
Equity reserve, not a P&L expense; should not be used for SG&A.

candidates.row_label: Amortization of Brands, Patents, Trademarks, Marketing & Artistic Intangibles
candidates.why: Amortization expense; per instructions D&A should be excluded from SG&A components even if related to marketing intangibles.

candidates.row_label: Amortization of Brands, Patents, Trademarks, Marketing & Artistic Intangibles - Supplemental
candidates.why: Supplemental amortization disclosure; D&A is excluded from SG&A components.

final_choice.row_label: Stock-Based Compensation in Selling, General & Administrative Expenses

variables.notes: Selected core operating overhead components and explicit SG&A stock-based compensation; excluded amortization per SG&A rules.

candidates.row_label: Amortization excluding Goodwill in Research & Development Expenses
candidates.why: Represents amortization within R&D, not the total R&D expense; using it as XRD would understate and misclassify R&D.

candidates.row_label: Licenses Patents and Acquired Research and Development - Gross
candidates.why: Balance sheet intangible asset balance, not period R&D expense.

candidates.row_label: Licenses Patents and Acquired Research and Development- Accumulated Amortization & Impairment
candidates.why: Accumulated amortization on intangibles; not current-period R&D expense.

candidates.row_label: Licenses Patents and Acquired Research and Development - Balancing value
candidates.why: Technical balancing line, not an expense.

variables.notes: No explicit R&D expense line is present; the only R&D-related row is amortization within R&D, which is not suitable as total R&D expense. XRD treated as 0.

candidates.why: Primary aggregate interest expense line; likely includes most borrowing costs and is the best top-level interest expense when available.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities
candidates.why: Interest on lease liabilities; a component of total interest expense but narrower than the main interest expense lines.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense - Net of Capitalized Interest
candidates.why: Core borrowing cost net of capitalized interest; good alternative measure of interest expense when the main 'Interest expenses' line is missing.
Represents allocation of profit to non-controlling interests, not interest on debt.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities - Supplemental
candidates.why: Supplemental detail of lease interest; overlapping with the main lease interest line and not needed if primary rows are used.
Balance sheet receivable; not an interest expense line.
Balance sheet receivable; not an expense.
Debt balance, not expense.
Current debt/interest-bearing liabilities; not P&L expense.
Current borrowings; not P&L expense.

final_choice.row_label: Interest Expense - Net of Capitalized Interest
Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities

variables.notes: Ordered as a priority list: main aggregate interest expenses first, then net-of-capitalized-interest measure, then lease interest as a fallback for years where others may be missing.

candidates.why: Component of equity, but not the full shareholders’ equity; better to use the total shareholders’ equity line for BE.
Represents equity attributable to shareholders (owners) and excludes non-controlling interests; ideal for book equity (BE).
Includes any minority interests; suitable only if no separate owners’ equity is available, which is not the case here.
Technical balancing line, not substantive equity amount.
Balancing adjustment, not the main equity figure.

candidates.row_label: Balancing Item - Total Liability and Equity
candidates.why: Technical balancing item; not equity itself.
Total of both sides of balance sheet; not pure equity.

variables.notes: Uses the shareholders’ equity total, which excludes minority interests and matches the BE definition.

candidates.why: Income statement allocation of profit to NCI; not the balance sheet minority equity but confirms presence of NCI concept.
Income statement minority interest; again not the balance sheet amount used in BE+MIB denominator.
Balance sheet line for minority/non-controlling interests; appropriate for MIB in the BE+MIB denominator, even if preview values are currently nan.

variables.notes: Mapped to the balance sheet minority/non-controlling interests total to pair with shareholders’ equity in BE.
